items.headline: US Markets Mixed as Geopolitical Tensions Weigh, Inflation Meets Expectations
items.summary: US stock market indices opened on a mixed note today, with the S&P 500 and tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite showing gains, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped. Investor sentiment remained fragile amidst escalating tensions in the Middle East, which kept crude oil prices elevated. Despite these geopolitical concerns, the latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) data for February came in line with expectations, offering a measure of stability on the inflation front. The International Energy Agency (IEA) announced a record release of emergency oil reserves to help calm surging prices.

items.headline: Oracle Soars on Strong Q3 Earnings and AI Demand
items.summary: Shares of Oracle Corp. ($ORCL) surged over 13% in early trade after reporting robust FQ3 results and guidance that surpassed Wall Street expectations. Revenue increased 22% year-over-year, primarily driven by a 44% rise in cloud revenue and an 84% surge in cloud infrastructure revenue. The company also highlighted a significant $553 billion backlog fueled by escalating AI demands, positioning it strongly in the global technology revolution.

items.headline: Ballard Power Systems Jumps on Major Fuel Cell Engine Deal
items.summary: Ballard Power Systems ($BLDP) saw its shares rise 15% following the announcement of a commercial agreement with New Flyer, part of NFI Group. The deal involves supplying 500 FCmove-HD+ fuel cell engines, totaling 50 MW, for zero-emission public transit buses across North America.

items.headline: Nebius Rises on Nvidia Investment and AI Partnership
items.summary: Nebius ($NBIS) shares gained 15% after Nvidia ($NVDA) announced a $2 billion investment and a strategic partnership aimed at developing next-generation hyperscale AI cloud infrastructure.

items.headline: Procter & Gamble Among Dow Decliners
items.summary: Procter & Gamble ($PG) was among the declining firms impacting the Dow Jones Industrial Average today, slipping 2.06%.

items.headline: Sherwin-Williams Sees Decline
items.summary: Shares of Sherwin-Williams ($SHW) experienced a decline of 1.67% today, contributing to the broader downturn in the Dow Jones.

items.headline: Caterpillar Down Amid Market Headwinds
items.summary: Caterpillar ($CAT) saw its stock fall by 1.55% as broader market pressures and geopolitical concerns affected industrial stocks.

items.headline: February CPI Aligns with Expectations, Inflation Steady Ahead of Conflict Escalation
items.summary: The U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) for February 2026 increased 0.3% month-over-month on a seasonally adjusted basis, meeting consensus estimates. The annual inflation rate stood at 2.4%, consistent with expectations and suggesting inflation had eased before the recent escalation of the Middle East conflict. Core CPI, excluding food and energy, rose 0.2% monthly and 2.5% annually, also in line with forecasts.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Drive Oil Volatility, IEA Releases Record Reserves
items.summary: Ongoing tensions in the Middle East, particularly the Iran crisis, continued to fuel volatility in crude oil prices and global energy markets. The International Energy Agency (IEA) announced its largest-ever release of strategic oil reserves, 400 million barrels, in an effort to curb surging prices and stabilize supply. The G7 nations also voiced support for using strategic oil reserves. Concerns remain regarding disruptions to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, with UNCTAD warning of widespread impacts on global supply chains and food prices.

items.headline: Geopolitical Events Reshape Sector Performance: Energy and Utilities Lead, Financials Lag
items.summary: The ongoing Middle East conflict is significantly reshuffling sector performance. Over the past month, the Utilities and Energy sectors have emerged as notable winners, driven by tightening global energy supplies and demand for safety in economically uncertain times. Conversely, the Financials sector has been identified as a significant laggard, showing weakness even before the recent geopolitical events. This indicates a broader rotation out of growth and into value stocks.

items.headline: Key Companies Report Earnings, Oracle Shines
items.summary: Today saw several companies reporting earnings, with Oracle ($ORCL) being a standout performer due to strong cloud growth and AI demand. Other major companies scheduled to report earnings today included Campbell Soup ($CPB), UiPath ($PATH), Descartes Systems Group Inc ($DSGX), Petco Health and Wellness Company ($WOOF), Bumble ($BMBL), and Stitch Fix ($SFIX), among others.
